# Code of Conduct

## Our Pledge
We are committed to making participation in this project a harassment-free and welcoming experience for everyone, regardless of level of experience, gender, gender identity and expression, sexual orientation, disability, personal appearance, body size, race, ethnicity, age, religion, or nationality.

We expect a community built on mutual respect, constructive feedback, and collaboration.

## Our Standards

### Examples of behavior that contributes to a positive environment:
* **Be Kind:** Treat fellow contributors, users, and maintainers with empathy and respect.
* **Be Constructive:** Focus on code, architecture, and documentation improvements. Gracefully accept and provide constructive criticism.
* **Be Inclusive:** Encourage newcomers and help answer questions clearly, avoiding gatekeeping or elitism.
* **Look Out for Each Other:** Focus on what is best for the project and the community as a whole.

### Examples of unacceptable behavior:
* Trolling, insulting, derogatory comments, and personal or political attacks.
* Public or private harassment, sexualized language, imagery, or unwelcome attention.
* Publishing others' private information (such as a physical or electronic address) without explicit permission.
* Other conduct which could reasonably be considered inappropriate in a professional or open-source setting.

## Enforcement Responsibilities
Project maintainers are responsible for clarifying and enforcing our standards of acceptable behavior. Maintainers have the right and responsibility to remove, edit, or reject comments, commits, code, wiki edits, issues, and other contributions that are not aligned to this Code of Conduct.

## Reporting & Scope
This Code of Conduct applies both within project spaces and in public spaces when an individual is representing the project or its community.

If you encounter or witness behavior that violates these standards, please report it by opening an issue or contacting the repository owner directly at **78921007+JS195@users.noreply.github.com**. All complaints will be reviewed and investigated promptly and fairly.

## Attribution
This Code of Conduct is adapted from the [Contributor Covenant][homepage], version 2.1, available at https://www.contributor-covenant.org/version/2/1/code_of_conduct.html
